name: Maine SNAP Benefits
description: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program providing monthly food benefits via EBT card to eligible low-income Maine households through the DHHS.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal income and resource guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 185% FPL under broad-based categorical eligibility

name: MaineCare (Maine Medicaid)
description: State Medicaid program providing health coverage for low-income Mainers including managed care and fee-for-service options for eligible populations.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income children, pregnant women, adults up to 138% FPL, elderly, and disabled individuals
incomeLimitNotes: Adults up to 138% FPL; children up to 213% FPL; pregnant women up to 214% FPL

name: Maine Department of Labor - Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial benefits to Maine workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own while they actively seek new employment.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost employment through no fault of their own and meet wage requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Must have earned sufficient wages during the base period

name: MaineHousing (Maine State Housing Authority)
description: State housing finance agency providing affordable mortgage programs, rental assistance, homelessness prevention, Section 8 vouchers, and housing counseling statewide.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income individuals and families seeking affordable housing
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally up to 80% area median income

name: Pine Tree Legal Assistance
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income Mainers statewide in matters including housing, family law, public benefits, consumer issues, and education.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Maine residents with civil legal needs
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 125% of federal poverty level

name: Maine CareerCenters
description: State workforce development centers providing job search assistance, resume help, career counseling, training programs, and employer connections across Maine.
eligibilitySummary: Any Maine resident seeking employment or career development services

name: Maine LIHEAP / Home Energy Assistance Program (HEAP)
description: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program helping eligible Maine households pay heating costs, which is particularly critical during Maine's harsh winters.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households needing help with heating costs
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 150% of federal poverty level

name: Maine 211
description: Free statewide information and referral service connecting Mainers with health and human services resources around the clock.
eligibilitySummary: Any Maine resident seeking help or information about available services

name: Good Shepherd Food Bank
description: Feeding America affiliate and Maine's largest hunger-relief organization distributing food through over 600 partner agencies statewide including pantries, shelters, and meal sites.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families in need of food assistance across Maine

name: Maine Bureau of Veterans' Services
description: Assists Maine veterans and their families with benefits claims, healthcare referrals, education, emergency financial assistance, and access to the Maine Veterans Homes.
eligibilitySummary: Maine veterans and their families

name: Maine Crisis Line
description: Statewide crisis line providing immediate support, crisis intervention, and referrals for individuals experiencing mental health or emotional crises.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Maine experiencing a mental health crisis or emotional distress

name: Maine Child Care Assistance (CCAP)
description: Subsidized childcare program helping low-income working Maine families afford quality childcare through the Temporary Assistance and Child Care program.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income working families with children needing childcare
incomeLimitNotes: Family income at or below 85% of state median income

name: Maine WIC Program
description: Women, Infants, and Children nutrition program providing supplemental foods, nutrition counseling, and health referrals at clinics statewide.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, breastfeeding, and postpartum women; infants and children under 5
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% of federal poverty level

name: Maine TANF Cash Assistance
description: Temporary Assistance for Needy Families providing cash benefits and work-related support to very low-income Maine families with dependent children.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families with dependent children
incomeLimitNotes: Income must be below TANF payment standard for family size

name: Metro Breez / METRO Portland Bus
description: Public bus system serving the Portland metropolitan area with local routes, express service, and connections to surrounding communities.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in the Portland metro area; reduced fare for seniors, disabled, and Medicare cardholders

name: Maine Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides free home energy efficiency improvements to low-income Maine households to reduce heating costs, which is essential given Maine's cold climate.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: Maine Adult Education
description: Free adult education programs including GED/HiSET preparation, English language classes, college transition, and workforce training at centers statewide.
eligibilitySummary: Adults age 16 and older not enrolled in K-12 school
hours: Varies by location; many evening classes

name: Maine Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Statewide hotline providing crisis intervention, safety planning, and shelter referrals for survivors of domestic violence through the Maine Coalition to End Domestic Violence.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing or at risk of domestic violence in Maine

name: Portland Housing Authority
description: Provides public housing and Section 8 vouchers to low-income families, elderly, and disabled residents in Portland, Maine's largest city.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in the Portland area
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 80% area median income for public housing; 50% for vouchers

name: Maine Vocational Rehabilitation
description: Provides employment services for Mainers with disabilities including vocational counseling, training, job placement, and assistive technology to achieve employment goals.
eligibilitySummary: Maine residents with physical or mental disabilities that impact employment

name: CubaCare / Maine CHIP (Children's Health Insurance)
description: Children's health insurance coverage through MaineCare for uninsured children in families earning too much for standard Medicaid eligibility.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured children under 19 in families with income too high for standard Medicaid
incomeLimitNotes: Family income up to 213% of federal poverty level

name: CoverME.gov - Maine Health Insurance Marketplace
description: Maine's state-based health insurance marketplace where residents can compare and enroll in qualified health plans with potential premium subsidies.
eligibilitySummary: Maine residents without employer coverage or other qualifying health insurance
incomeLimitNotes: Premium tax credits available for households between 100-400% FPL
hours: Monday-Friday 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM ET; extended during open enrollment

name: Maine Lifeline Telephone Assistance
description: Provides discounted phone and broadband service to eligible low-income Maine residents to ensure access to essential telecommunications.
eligibilitySummary: Households participating in SNAP, Medicaid, SSI, or with income at or below 135% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 135% FPL or participation in qualifying federal program

name: Maine Community College System - Workforce Training
description: Workforce development and career training programs through Maine's community colleges including certificates, apprenticeships, and customized employer training.
eligibilitySummary: Adults seeking career training and workforce education
incomeLimitNotes: Free community college for eligible Maine residents under Maine program

name: Maine Office of Aging and Disability Services
description: Provides services for seniors and people with disabilities including home-based care, meals, transportation, respite, and long-term care coordination.
eligibilitySummary: Maine residents age 60+, individuals with disabilities, and their caregivers
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program

name: Volunteer Lawyers Project of Maine
description: Pro bono legal referral program matching low-income Mainers with volunteer attorneys for civil legal matters at no cost.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Maine residents with civil legal needs who cannot afford an attorney
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: Community Concepts - Western Maine Community Action
description: Community action agency serving western Maine with fuel assistance, weatherization, Head Start, housing, transportation, and emergency assistance programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of western Maine
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally at or below 200% FPL

name: Penquis - Central/Eastern Maine Community Action
description: Community action agency serving central and eastern Maine with energy assistance, housing, childcare, transportation, and workforce development programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of central and eastern Maine
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally at or below 200% FPL

name: Bangor Housing Authority
description: Provides public housing and Section 8 vouchers to low-income families, elderly, and disabled residents in the Bangor area.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in the Bangor area
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 80% area median income

name: Maine General Assistance Program
description: Municipal-level emergency assistance program providing help with basic necessities like housing, food, utilities, and medication for those in immediate need.
eligibilitySummary: Maine residents in immediate need who have exhausted other resources
incomeLimitNotes: Must demonstrate insufficient income and resources to meet basic needs

name: Western Maine Transportation Services
description: Public transit and demand-response transportation serving rural western Maine communities with rides to medical appointments, shopping, and other essential trips.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in western Maine; priority for seniors, disabled, and low-income residents

name: Preble Street - Portland Homeless Services
description: Comprehensive services for people experiencing homelessness and poverty in the Portland area including day shelter, meals, case management, and housing programs.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families experiencing homelessness or poverty in the Portland area
hours: Varies by program; resource center open daily

name: Maine Free College Program
description: State program providing free community college tuition for eligible Maine residents at all seven Maine community colleges.
eligibilitySummary: Maine residents who graduated high school 2020 or later and meet eligibility requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Last-dollar program after other financial aid; no income limit for recent graduates
